title: Change RerankerSpecter Dataset question key
body:

# GitHub Actions CI

The Jenkins CI system has been replaced by GitHub Actions self-hosted runners.

The GitHub Actions CI will run automatically when the "Run CICD" label is added to the PR.
To re-run CI remove and add the label again.
To run CI on an untrusted fork, a NeMo user with write access must first click "Approve and run".

# Before your PR is "Ready for review"

**Pre checks**:

- [ ] Make sure you read and followed [Contributor guidelines](https://github.com/NVIDIA/NeMo/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md)
- [ ] Did you write any new necessary tests?
- [ ] Did you add or update any necessary documentation?
- [ ] Does the PR affect components that are optional to install? (Ex: Numba, Pynini, Apex etc)
  - [ ] Reviewer: Does the PR have correct import guards for all optional libraries?
  
**PR Type**:

- [ ] New Feature
- [ ] Bugfix
- [ ] Documentation

If you haven't finished some of the above items you can still open "Draft" PR.

## Who can review?

Anyone in the community is free to review the PR once the checks have passed.
[Contributor guidelines](https://github.com/NVIDIA/NeMo/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) contains specific people who can review PRs to various areas.

# Additional Information

- Related to # (issue)

step_plan.summary: Fix a key mismatch for the SPECTER reranker dataset by updating SpecterReRankerDataModule._create_dataset to use question_key='query' so the dataset loader reads the 'query' field produced by SPECTER preprocessing and avoids KeyError at runtime.

steps.operation: Change the question_key passed to the factory when creating the Specter reranker dataset
steps.reason: Specter preprocessing writes preprocessed JSONL records with the key 'query' for the question text. SpecterReRankerDataModule currently calls create_reranker_dataset(..., question_key='question', ...), causing ReRankerDataset to look for a non-existent 'question' key and raising a KeyError. Passing question_key='query' (or removing the override to use the factory default 'query') aligns the DataModule with the actual file format and fixes dataset construction at runtime.
steps.side_effects:
Low-risk, one-line change. Potential impacts:
- Tests: Add or update unit/integration tests to ensure the Specter DataModule reads examples without KeyError; run reranker-related test suites to catch regressions.
- Dependents: Any code that (incorrectly) expected 'question' keys in Specter preprocessed files will break — but current Specter preprocessing and tests expect 'query', so this change restores intended behavior.
- API/Backwards compatibility: No public API change; only internal wiring between Specter DataModule and create_reranker_dataset. If other collections or external tooling relied on the wrong 'question' field, those would need updating (unlikely).
- Future maintenance: There are inconsistent default names across layers (create_reranker_dataset default vs ReRankerDataset.__init__ vs custom DataModule defaults). This step keeps change minimal; a larger harmonization of defaults is recommended separately.
